content_markdown: Gravitational potential at a point is defined as the work done

options.label: A
options.text: per unit mass in moving a small mass from infinity to the point.

options.label: B
options.text: in moving a unit mass from infinity to the point.

options.label: C
options.text: in moving a small mass from infinity to the point.

options.label: D
options.text: per unit mass in moving a unit mass from infinity to the point.

vibe_explanation: Gravitational potential ($V$) is defined as the work done per unit mass ($W/m$) in bringing a small test mass from infinity to a point in the gravitational field. Option A correctly identifies the 'per unit mass' requirement while specifying that the mass being moved is a small test mass to avoid disturbing the field source.
